0.body: Here is describing Ursus. Ursus is language which is embeded to Coq. This language was created for describing solidity smart contracts. It has some key features such as: easy descirbing and understanding logic of programs, translators (from, to solidity).
0.title: Introduction

1.body: Contract file structure Contract file consist of five parts: Headers. Headers contains required libraries, required work files (for ex. files with interfaces) and other (i.e. scopes) Interfaces. Some interfaces are more convenient to write not in separate files, but in the contract file itself Contract. Here the elements of the contract are described: records, constants and fields of the contract. List of used types. Contract functions. Here the function realization are describes.
1.breadcrumbs: Ursus as a language » Contract file structure
1.title: Ursus as a language

10.body: For each function, its structure is as follows: AccessRights Key words 'Ursus Definition' FunctionName ArgumentsList ':' Key word 'UExpression' ReturnType [true', 'false'] '.' FuctionBody Key word 'Defined' '.' AccessRight : #[ar1] or # [ar1 , ar2] ar may be: external, public, private, pure, nonpayable FunctionName : One word without spaces, starts with either a letter or an underscore. May contain numbers. ArgumentsList : for function without arguments - easy, For one argument : (ArgumentName ':' ArgumentType) For multiple arguments : (Argumen1tName ':' Argument1Type) ... (ArgumentNName ':' ArgumentNType) ArgumentName : One word without spaces, starts with either a letter or an underscore. May contain numbers Argument1Type : see "Types, primitives and literals" . For a function that does not return a value : ReturnType is 'PhantomType' Type of return value : see "Types, primitives and literals" . ['true', 'false'] : 'true' for function: or function contains 'require' operator(s); or function calls function(s) with 'true'; or function contains an intermediate exit from the function (most often this is an 'return' by the if operator) 'false' - in other cases FunctionBody - set of operators that make up the function see "Function operators" .
10.breadcrumbs: Ursus as a language » Functions and modifiers
10.title: Ursus as a language

11.body: Function operators Each line must start with a character ' :: ' or character ' : ' . If the character is ' :: ' then no reverse translation is performed. If the character is ' : ' the reverse translation will be performed. Followed by the symbol ' // '. Exception to this - see below. Unlike other programming languages, in Ursus any constructs can be replaced with a " hole " that can be filled in later. In some cases (which are discussed below), these " holes " need to be forced to be typed. The method of using " holes " allows you to decompose the most complex structures into components, which can greatly simplify the programming process. Creating a new variable There are six kinds of operators for creating new variables: ::// new 'varname : vartype @ "varname" ; _ | . ?::// new 'varname : vartype ; _ | . :// new 'varname : vartype @ "varname" ; _ | . ?:// new 'varname : vartype ; _ | . ::// new 'varname : vartype @ "varname" := ... ; _ | . ?::// new 'varname : vartype := ... ; _ | . :// new 'varname : vartype @ "varname" := ... ; _ | . ?:// new 'varname : vartype := ... ; _ | . ::// new ('var1name : var1type , .. , 'varNname : varNtype) @ ("var1name" , .. , "varNname") ; _ |. ?::// new ('var1name : var1type , .. , 'varNname : varNtype) ; _ |. ::// new ('var1name : var1type , .. , 'varNname : varNtype) @ ("var1name" , .. , "varNname") := ... ; _ |. ?::// new ('var1name : var1type , .. , 'varNname : varNtype) := ... ; _ |. :// new ('var1name : var1type , .. , 'varNname : varNtype) @ ("var1name" , .. , "varNname") ; _ |. ?:// new ('var1name : var1type , .. , 'varNname : varNtype) ; _ |. :// new ('var1name : var1type , .. , 'varNname : varNtype) @ ("var1name" , .. , "varNname") := ... ; _ |. ?:// new ('var1name : var1type , .. , 'varNname : varNtype) := ... ; _ |. where '::', '?::' - no reverse broadcast ':', '?:' - reverse translation '//' - Ursus interpreter ';' - binding '_' - "hole", i.e. there must be a continuation after the operator 'new' ... - an expression that returns the value(s) assigned to the new variable(s) For examples: ::// new 'sender : ( uint256 ) @ "sender" := msg->pubkey() ; _ | . ?:// new ( 'unlocked : uint128 , 'unlockedPeriod : uint32 ) ; _ |. To read the value of an argument in the function body, use the sign '#': :// a := #b . - here b is argument of function To read the value of a local variable in the function body, use the sign '!': :// a := !b . - here b is local variable, a is local variable too Assignment Regular assignment operator ':=' : left expression ':=' right expression '.' For record members ... - TODO For examples: :://valueL := (β #{0}) . ://needCleanup := (!updateId <= !marker) . Numeric constants To use unnamed (numerical) constants in the program text, special characters are used: For ' require_ ' operator: % ... For all other cases: (β #{ ... }) For examples: ://valueL := (β #{0}) . ://require_(m_custodians->exists(msg->pubkey()), %100) . Arithmetic operators ' + ', ' - ', '*', ' / ', ' ++ ', ' -- ', ' & ', ' | ', '<<' '>>' For examples: :// i ++ . :://return_ (#mask - (!onee << (!eight * #index ))) |. Comparison Operators '>', '<', '>=', '<=', '==' For examples: !i < MAX_CLEANUP_TXNS : ://needCleanup := (!trId <= !marker) |.* Logical operators ' || ', ' && ' For example: (!{i} < !{len}) && (!{ownerCount} < MAX_CUSTODIAN_COUNT) Combined assignment ' += ', ' -= ', '*=', ' /= ', ' &= ', ' |= ' For example: ::// maskL |= (!{onee} << #custodianIndex ) . Function call Conditional operator Two kinds of conditional statement: 'if' (condition) 'then' { statement } . 'if' (condition) 'then' { statement1 } 'else' { statement2 } . It is convenient to use " holes " for the conditional operator. For example: ::// if ( m_updateRequests->empty() ) then { {_:UExpression _ true} } . :://exit_ {} |. The construction ' {_:UExpression _ true} ' means that a " hole " is specified and its type is specified - coinciding with the type of the expression that is written later. In this case, this is exit {} |. The vertical line ' | ' indicates that the decoding of the " hole " is completed. Loop operators There are two kinds of loop statements: ' while ' and ' for '. An unindexed loop looks like this: 'while' condition 'do' statement. The cycle for looks like this: 'for' condition do ststement . For example: ://while ((!{needCleanup} && (!{i} < MAX_CLEANUP_TXNS))) do { {_:UExpression _ true} } |. ::// for ( 'pubkey : #{claimers} ) do { {_:UExpression _ false} } . Record initialization The following construction is used to initialize records: '[$' statement1 '⇒' {Member1OfRecord}; ... statement2 '⇒' {Member2OfRecord} '$]' The order in which the members are listed is arbitrary. If some members are not listed, they will be initialized by default. For example: ::// new 'tmp : MultisigWallet_ι_UpdateRequestLRecord @ "tmp" := [$ !updateId ⇒ {MultisigWallet_ι_UpdateRequest_ι_id}; !index ⇒ {MultisigWallet_ι_UpdateRequest_ι_index}; (β #{0}) ⇒ {MultisigWallet_ι_UpdateRequest_ι_signs}; (β #{0}) ⇒ {MultisigWallet_ι_UpdateRequest_ι_confirmationsMask}; !sender ⇒ {MultisigWallet_ι_UpdateRequest_ι_creator}; codeHash ⇒ {MultisigWallet_ι_UpdateRequest_ι_codeHash}; #owners ⇒ {MultisigWallet_ι_UpdateRequest_ι_custodians}; #reqConfirms ⇒ {MultisigWallet_ι_UpdateRequest_ι_reqConfirms} $] ; _|. Here a local variable 'tmp' of type 'MultisigWallet_ι_UpdateRequestLRecord' is created and an initialized record is assigned to it. Message Library Functions

Type false get x ->get () URValue Type true <optional(Type)>.hasValue() umaybe_has_value x ->hasValue() URValue boolean false returns <optional(Type)>.set(Type value)?? umaybe_set x ->set() URValue (optional Type) b1 set x ->set (a) UExpression R (orb b2 b1) <optional(Type)>.reset(); stdFunc stdNotations returns reset x ->reset () UExpression R false vector(Type) <vector(Type)>.push(Type) vector_push__ x -> vector_push_(y) URValue (listVector Type) (orb b1 b2) vector_push_ x -> push (y) UExpression R (orb b2 b1) <vector(Type)>.pop() vector_pop__ x -> vector_pop_() URValue (listVector Type) (orb b1 b2) vector_pop_ x -> pop () UExpression R (orb b2 b1) <vector(Type)>.length() length x -> length () URValue uint b returns <vector(Type)>.empty() is_empty_ x ->empty() URValue boolean b 13.body: Here is a mapping of operators from solidity and the phased construction of operators in ursus Precedence Soldity stdFunc stdNotations(notations to use) level $1, $2, whole notations Comments 1 x++ 55, _, 55 1 x-- 55, _, 55 1 <array>[<index>] _, _, 55 1 <object>.<member> _, _, 55 1 <func>(<args>) _, _, _ 2 ++x _, _, 60 2 --x _, _, 60 2 delete operation _, 60, 60 2 ! x uneg " '!' x " _, _, 60 2 ~ x unot " '~' x " _, _, 60 3 a**b _, _, _ 4 x * y un_mult' " x '*' y " _, _, 70 4 x / y un_div' " x '/' y " _, _, 70 4 x % y un_mod' " x '%' y " _, 70, 70 5 x + y un_plus' " x '+' y " _, _, 75 5 x - y un_minus' " x '-' y " _, _, 75 6 x >> y un_right' " x '>>' y " _, 80, 80 6 x << y un_left' " x '<<' y " _, 80, 80 7 x & y un_and' " x '&' y " _, 85, 85 8 x ^ y un_xor' " x '^' y " _, 90, 90 9 x | y un_or' " x '\' y " _, 95, 95 10 x < y un_ltb " x '<' y " _, 100, 100 10 x <= y un_leb " x '<=' y " _, 100, 100 10 x > y un_gtb " x '>' y " _, 100, 100 10 x >= y un_geb " x '>=' y " _, 100, 100 11 x == y un_eqb " x '==' y " _, 105, 105 11 x != y un_uneqb " x '!=' y " _, 105, 105 12 x && y uband " x '&&' y " _, 110, 110 13 x || y ubor " x '\\' y " _, 115, 115 14 <conditional> ? <if-true> : <if-false> _, _, 125 // belongs UMLang library 14 := _, _, 125 // belongs UMLang library 14 |= _, _, 125 14 ^= _, _, _ //doesn't exist 14 &= _, _, 125 14 <<= _, _, _ //doesn't exists 14 >>= _, _, _ //doesn't exists 14 += _, _, 125 14 -= _, _, 125 14 *= _, _, _ //doesn't exist 14 /= _, _, _ //doesn't exist 14 %= _, _, _ //doesn't exist

2.body: The required contract file headers look like this: Block of standard libraries of the coq system: Require Import Coq.Program.Basics. Require Import Coq.Strings.String. Require Import Setoid. Require Import ZArith. Require Import QArith. Require Import Coq.Program.Equality. Require Import Lia. Block of ursus-specific libraries: Require Import FinProof.All. Require Import UMLang.All. Require Import UrsusStdLib.Solidity.All. Require Import UrsusStdLib.Solidity.unitsNotations. Require Import UrsusTVM.Solidity.All. Require Import UrsusTVM.Solidity.UrsusDefinitions. Require Import UrsusTVM.Solidity.ReverseTranslatorConstructions. Require Import UMLang.UrsusLib. Require Import UMLang.GlobalClassGenerator.ClassGenerator. Require Import UMLang.LocalClassGenerator.ClassGenerator. Import UrsusNotations. Scope connection block: Local Open Scope xlist_scope. Local Open Scope record. Local Open Scope program_scope. Local Open Scope glist_scope. Local Open Scope ursus_scope. Local Open Scope usolidity_scope. Local Open Scope struct_scope. Local Open Scope N_scope. Local Open Scope string_scope. Local Open Scope list_scope. Connecting the elpi system: From elpi Require Import elpi.
2.breadcrumbs: Ursus as a language » Contract file headers
2.title: Ursus as a language

3.body: The interfaces in the contract file itself have the same structure as in a separate file: Key word 'Interfaces.' Key word 'MakeInterface' key word 'Class' IntefaceName ':=' '{' Function1Name ':' Carryed List of Arguments Types '->' AccessRights ReturnType ['true', 'false'] ';' Function2Name ':' Carryed List of Arguments Types '->' AccessRights ReturType ['true', 'false'] ';' ... } Key word 'EndInterfaces.' For example: Let the Solidity interface look like this: interface IVestingPool { function claim(uint poolId, address addr) external; function get() external view returns(uint poolId, address poolCreator); } Then on Ursus it is written like this: Interfaces. MakeInterface Class IVestingPool := { claim : uint -> address -> external PhantomType true ; get : external (uint ** address) false ; }. EndInterfaces. The modificators 'true' and 'false' see in functions file.
3.breadcrumbs: Ursus as a language » Contract file interfaces
3.title: Ursus as a language

4.body: The structure of the contract as a record is as follows: Key word 'Contract' ContractName ';' Key words 'Send To' InterfacesNames ';' Key word 'Types' Key word 'Record' Name1Record ':= {' Name1RecordMemberName ':' Type1 ';' Name1RecordMemberName ':' Type2 ';' ... Name1RecordMemberName ':' TypeN } Key word 'Record' Name2Record ':= {' ... } ... Key word 'Record' NameLastRecord ':= {' ... } ';' Key word 'Constants' Key word 'Definition' Constant1Name ':' Type1 ':=' Key word 'Build_XUBInteger' value1 Key word 'Definition' Constant2Name ':' Type2 ':=' Key word 'Build_XUBInteger' value2 ... Key word 'Definition' ConstantLastName ':' TypeLast ':=' Key word 'Build_XUBInteger' valueLast ';' Key words 'Record Contract' ':= {' '#[' AccessRights ']' Name1ContractMember ':' Type1 ';' '#[' AccessRights ']' Name2ContractMember ':' Type2 ';' ... NameNContractMember ':' TypeN ';' ... NameLastContractMember ':' TypeLast '}.' For example: Contract MultisigWallet ; Sends To Itmp ; Types Record Transaction := { Transaction_ι_id : uint64; Transaction_ι_confirmationsMask : uint32; } Record UpdateRequest := { UpdateRequest_ι_id : uint64; UpdateRequest_ι_index : uint8; UpdateRequest_ι_signs : uint8; }; Constants Definition FLAG_SEND_ALL_REMAINING : uint8 := Build_XUBInteger (128) Definition FLAG_IGNORE_ERRORS : uint8 := Build_XUBInteger (2) Record Contract := { #[static] _pubkey : uint256; #[static] _foo : uint256; m_requiredVotes : uint8; m_defaultRequiredConfirmations : uint8 }.
4.breadcrumbs: Ursus as a language » Contract record
4.title: Ursus as a language

7.body: type Description boolean logical type for ( true and false ) address address type consist of two parts: analogue of Solidity address type mapping typeA typeB hash-map type: analogue of Solidity mapping type PhantomType empty type: for functions that do not return a value analogue of void C type ______________________________

9.body: Constants There are two types of constants. First is 'global', i.e. constants are outside the contract; and other ('local') are inside the contract. Global constants Local constants Local constants are inside the contract. Inside the contract constants definition looks like that: Key word 'Constants' Key word 'Definition' Constan1tName ':' Type1 ':=' Key word 'Build_XUBInteger' Value1 Key word 'Definition' Constan2tName ':' Type2 ':=' Key word 'Build_XUBInteger' Value2 Key word 'Definition' Constan3tName ':' Type3 ':=' Key word 'Build_XUBInteger' Value3 ; Attention: Semicolon (;) should be after last constant definition. For example: Definition ERR_ADDR_ZERO : uint16 := Build_XUBInteger 104 Definition ERR_LOW_AMOUNT : uint16 := Build_XUBInteger 105 Definition ERR_LOW_BALANCE : uint16 := Build_XUBInteger 106 Definition ERR_NOT_SELF : uint16 := Build_XUBInteger 107 ;
9.breadcrumbs: Ursus as a language » Global constants
9.title: Ursus as a language
